Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: List Airtable bases using airtable_list_bases to discover the MoveOps operations base (appMoveOpsOps001). Then list tables using airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01).
OE 2: List or search Airtable records in the Relocations table using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Status, value: Completed) to find all records with Status "Completed". Among the April-dated results, find 15 records: Dr. Yusuf Abdi (recReloc00000001, Canopy Health, Minneapolis to Chicago, Apr 18-19), Lily Marchetti (recReloc00000002, Canopy Health, Nashville to Chicago, Apr 25-26), Kevin Tran (recReloc00000003, Vectral Systems, Portland to Denver, Apr 19-20), Danielle Osei (recReloc00000004, Vectral Systems, Phoenix to Denver, Apr 26-27), Priya Venkatesh (recReloc00000005, GreenStack Energy, Houston to Seattle, Apr 21-23), Noah Fitzgerald (recReloc00000006, GreenStack Energy, Philadelphia to Seattle, Apr 28-29), Ines Kovac (recReloc00000007, PivotPoint Labs, Brooklyn to Austin, Apr 22-24), Jordan Ekwueme (recReloc00000008, BrightLoop Analytics, San Diego to Boston, Apr 25-26), Andre Washington (recReloc00000009, Mosaic Robotics, Detroit to Atlanta, Apr 26-27), Tom Lindqvist (recReloc00000024, Greenleaf Design Studio, Portland to Denver, Apr 7-9), Daniela Cruz (recReloc00000025, Sunbelt Venture Partners, Chicago to Miami, Apr 14-16), Marco Silva (recReloc00000026, Tideway Hospitality Tech, Tampa to Austin, Apr 7-9), Dana Kowalczyk (recReloc00000027, Tideway Hospitality Tech, Tampa to Seattle, Apr 10-12), Ravi Sundaram (recReloc00000028, Tideway Hospitality Tech, Tampa to Denver, Apr 14-16), and Zara Okoye (recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta, Mosaic Robotics, Savannah to Atlanta, Apr 14-16). Note that multiple records have modified_time set well before their Move End Date: among the first 9, Marchetti, Osei, Fitzgerald, Ekwueme, and Washington were all modified in early April but have move dates in late April. All 6 additional records (Lindqvist, Cruz, Silva, Kowalczyk, Sundaram, Okoye) also have modified_time equal to their created_time, set before their move dates. This premature completion marking is an early signal that the Completed status may not reflect actual move outcomes.
OE 3: Search emails for Lily Marchetti using search_emails (query: "Lily Marchetti" or "Marchetti"). Search both INBOX and SENT folders (folder_name: "INBOX" then folder_name: "SENT") since cancellation emails were sent by MoveOps staff. In INBOX, find the original relocation request from Patricia Langford (email_canopy_marchetti_intro) and Lily's move details (email_canopy_marchetti_details), plus the Heartland Q1 invoice from Jake Loomis (email_heartland_q1_invoice_submission) listing Marchetti as a billed move. In SENT, discover Chloe Vance's cancellation email to Jake Loomis at Heartland Movers (email_cancel_marchetti_heartland, subject: "Cancellation — Lily Marchetti Move (Nashville → Chicago) — RELOC-2026-009") sent February 10, 2026, stating the employee decided not to relocate. Also find Chloe's review email (email_chloe_heartland_review_findings) listing Marchetti as a disputed charge on Heartland's Q1 invoice, and Marcus Thorne's formal dispute letter (email_marcus_dispute_heartland_q1) referencing Marchetti as one of 3 invalid line items on invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042.
OE 4: Search emails for Danielle Osei using search_emails (query: "Danielle Osei" or "Osei"). Search both INBOX and SENT folders (folder_name: "INBOX" then folder_name: "SENT") since cancellation emails were sent by MoveOps staff. In INBOX, find Rachel Whitfield's relocation request (email_vectral_osei_intro) and Danielle's move details (email_vectral_osei_details). In SENT, discover Chloe Vance's cancellation email to Jake Loomis at Heartland Movers (email_cancel_osei_heartland, subject: "Cancellation — Danielle Osei Move (Phoenix → Denver) — RELOC-2026-010") sent February 13, 2026, stating Vectral Systems pulled the relocation request due to budget reallocation. Also find Chloe's invoice review and Marcus's dispute letter referencing Osei as another invalid line item.
OE 5: Search Slack for messages about the Heartland invoice dispute or Marchetti/Osei cancellations using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Marchetti" or "Osei cancellation" or "Heartland dispute" or "cancelled") to find Chloe's vendor billing discrepancy post in #operations (C006) identifying three disputed charges on the Heartland Q1 invoice: Marchetti ($1,600, cancelled), Osei ($1,600, cancelled), and Venkatesh ($1,600, move completed by Swift Relocations not Heartland) — totaling $4,800 in overbilling out of $12,800 invoiced. Also find Marcus's payment hold post in #finance (C005) listing all three as disputed charges on invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 and placing a payment hold on the full $12,800 until a corrected bill is received.
OE 6: Optionally discover the second set of Airtable records for Marchetti and Osei by searching airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Name, value: "Lily Marchetti") or browsing additional records. Find rec_reloc_marchetti with Status "Cancelled" and special requirements noting "CANCELLED — Employee decided not to relocate. Heartland Movers was initially booked but cancellation sent to Jake Loomis on 2/10." Similarly find rec_reloc_osei with Status "Cancelled" noting "CANCELLED — Client pulled relocation request due to budget reallocation. Cancellation email sent to Heartland (Jake Loomis) on 2/13."
OE 7: Search emails for the remaining 7 relocatees from the first 9 using search_emails with queries like "Yusuf Abdi", "Kevin Tran", "Priya Venkatesh", "Noah Fitzgerald", "Ines Kovac", "Jordan Ekwueme", and "Andre Washington". For 4 of the 7 -- Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Kevin Tran, Priya Venkatesh, and Ines Kovac -- find relocation request threads, apartment selection confirmations, flight bookings, and coordinator correspondence with no signals contradicting their Completed status. Their Airtable modified_time aligns with their move end dates, and email threads show standard successful completion workflows. For Andre Washington, discover conflicting information: one email thread (email_mosaic_washington_intro, email_mosaic_washington_reply, email_mosaic_washington_details) describes his relocation as Detroit, MI to Atlanta, GA (matching Airtable), but a parallel set of emails (email_relocation_request_001, email_am_ack_001, email_flight_request_001, email_flight_confirm_001, email_client_confirm_001) describes the same person relocating from Columbus, GA to Seattle, WA with flights booked CMH to SEA -- a major geography contradiction. For Noah Fitzgerald, find planning and booking emails (apartment options, flight booking PHL to SEA) but no evidence of actual move completion -- his move dates (Apr 28-29) have not yet occurred as of the current date (April 26, 2026), and his record was marked Completed on April 2. For Jordan Ekwueme, find temp housing coordination and planning emails but note that his move end date is April 26 (today) and the record was marked Completed on April 1, well before the move could have finished.
OE 8: Search emails and Slack for the 6 additional April relocatees using search_emails and conversations_search_messages. For Zara Okoye (Mosaic Robotics, Savannah to Atlanta), discover extensive email threads documenting a major prototype damage incident: Tamara Byrd's formal damage complaint reporting a cracked servo motor housing and misaligned joint calibration on a $90,000 robotic arm prototype (Unit #1) upon delivery on April 15, Emeka Diallo's incident response and full chain-of-custody report to Tamara, and related Slack escalation threads in #operations (C006) including Chloe Vance's chain-of-custody investigation, a delivery receipt signed by an intern (Kyle Britten) instead of Zara, and Marcus Thorne's financial analysis identifying a $40,000 uncovered insurance gap. Although Zara's relocation was physically completed, the move was NOT clean -- the prompt instruction "I don't want to email someone congratulating them if their move is still messy behind the scenes" applies directly to this case. For the remaining 5 additional relocatees -- Tom Lindqvist (Greenleaf Design Studio), Daniela Cruz (Sunbelt Venture Partners), Marco Silva, Dana Kowalczyk, and Ravi Sundaram (all Tideway Hospitality Tech) -- search emails and Slack and find NO correspondence, NO relocation threads, and NO cross-service data to verify their Completed status. Their Airtable records exist but cannot be corroborated through any other service, making cross-checking impossible as required by the prompt.
OE 9: Search Slack for discussions about the remaining 7 relocatees from the first 9 using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Abdi" or "Venkatesh" or "Kovac" or "Fitzgerald" or "Ekwueme" or "Washington" or "Tran"). Discover the Q1/Early Q2 Relocation Volume Update post in #operations (C006) which lists all 9 April relocations with status indicators: Abdi to Chicago (fully booked), Tran to Denver (fully booked), Venkatesh to Seattle (fully booked), Kovac to Austin (fully booked), Marchetti to Chicago (in progress), Osei to Denver (in progress), Fitzgerald to Seattle (in progress), Ekwueme to Boston (in progress), and Washington to Atlanta (in progress). The in-progress markers for Marchetti and Osei provide additional Slack confirmation of their non-completion (corroborating the cancellation evidence from OEs 3-5). The in-progress markers for Fitzgerald, Ekwueme, and Washington directly contradict the Airtable "Completed" status for those three records. Also find the "Two Noahs" confusion thread in #operations where Fatimah asks whether a new intake is "Fitzpatrick or Fitzgerald" -- confirming parallel Seattle moves that could cause mix-ups. For the 4 clean relocatees (Abdi, Tran, Venkatesh, Kovac), find no negative signals in Slack.
OE 10: Update the Lily Marchetti Airtable record to fix the incorrect Completed status using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: recReloc00000002, fields: {Status: "Cancelled"}}]). This corrects the false Completed status based on clear cancellation evidence from emails and the second Airtable record.
OE 11: Update the Danielle Osei Airtable record to fix the incorrect Completed status using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: recReloc00000004, fields: {Status: "Cancelled"}}]). This corrects the false Completed status based on clear cancellation evidence from emails and the second Airtable record.
OE 12: Flag Omar Ibrahim and Julian Brooks about the uncertain records where the evidence is ambiguous, conflicting, or absent rather than conclusive. Use send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com, recipients: ["omar.ibrahim@moveops.com", "julian.brooks@moveops.com"]) or conversations_add_message to a DM. The flag should cover: (a) Andre Washington (recReloc00000009) -- Airtable says Detroit, MI to Atlanta, GA but multiple email threads describe his relocation as Columbus, GA to Seattle, WA with flights booked CMH to SEA; the geography data is contradictory across email and Airtable and the record was marked Completed on April 2 despite a move end date of April 27; (b) Noah Fitzgerald (recReloc00000006) -- move dates are April 28-29 which have not yet occurred, the Slack volume post marks him as in progress, the record was marked Completed on April 2 (26 days before the move starts), and there is a "Two Noahs" confusion risk with a concurrent Axiom/Fitzpatrick Seattle move; (c) Jordan Ekwueme (recReloc00000008) -- the Slack volume post marks him as in progress, the record was marked Completed on April 1 despite a move end date of April 26, and his stipend for a monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) is still Pending Approval with a PDF template fraud flag; (d) Zara Okoye (recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta) -- relocation physically completed but a $90,000 robotic arm prototype was damaged in transit with an ongoing insurance dispute and $40K uncovered gap, so a congratulatory check-in would be inappropriate given the active damage claim; (e) Tom Lindqvist (recReloc00000024), Daniela Cruz (recReloc00000025), Marco Silva (recReloc00000026), Dana Kowalczyk (recReloc00000027), and Ravi Sundaram (recReloc00000028) -- all marked Completed with premature modified_times but no email, Slack, or contact data exists to cross-verify their status, making the prompt's required cross-check impossible. Per the prompt instruction: "If you are not sure, flag me and Julian with what you found instead of editing."
OE 13: Look up contact information for each of the 4 verified clean relocatees using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Yusuf Abdi"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Kevin Tran"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Priya Venkatesh"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Ines Kovac") to retrieve their email addresses before sending check-in emails. Expected addresses: yusuf.abdi@canopyhealth.io, kevin.tran@vectralsystems.com, priya.venkatesh@greenstackenergy.com, ines@pivotpointlabs.com.
OE 14: Send check-in emails from Omar Ibrahim to each of the 4 verified clean relocatees using send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com). Each email should be a short, friendly check-in asking how they are settling in and whether they need anything. Recipients: yusuf.abdi@canopyhealth.io, kevin.tran@vectralsystems.com, priya.venkatesh@greenstackenergy.com, ines@pivotpointlabs.com. Emails should NOT be sent to Lily Marchetti or Danielle Osei (relocations cancelled), should NOT be sent to Noah Fitzgerald, Jordan Ekwueme, or Andre Washington (flagged as uncertain -- awaiting review by Omar and Julian), should NOT be sent to Zara Okoye (active damage incident makes a congratulatory check-in inappropriate), and should NOT be sent to the 5 additional relocatees (Lindqvist, Cruz, Silva, Kowalczyk, Sundaram) whose status cannot be cross-verified.
OE 15: Post a summary message in #customer-support using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C007) listing: (a) who was contacted with check-in emails (Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Kevin Tran, Priya Venkatesh, Ines Kovac -- 4 relocatees whose moves checked out clean across Airtable, email, and Slack); (b) which records were corrected (Lily Marchetti changed from Completed to Cancelled based on cancellation emails and second Airtable record, Danielle Osei changed from Completed to Cancelled based on cancellation emails and second Airtable record); (c) which records were flagged for Omar and Julian's review (Andre Washington -- conflicting geography across Airtable and email, Noah Fitzgerald -- move dates not yet reached and Slack shows in progress, Jordan Ekwueme -- Slack shows in progress with pending stipend fraud flag, Zara Okoye -- move completed but $90K prototype damaged in transit with active insurance dispute, and 5 additional relocatees with no cross-service data available for verification); and (d) the evidence basis for each correction and flag.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: List Airtable bases using airtable_list_bases to discover the MoveOps operations base (appMoveOpsOps001). Then list tables using airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01).
OE 2: List or search Airtable records in the Relocations table using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) or airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Status, value: Completed) to find all records with Status "Completed". Among the April-dated results, find 15 records: Dr. Yusuf Abdi (recReloc00000001, Canopy Health, Minneapolis to Chicago, Apr 18-19), Lily Marchetti (recReloc00000002, Canopy Health, Nashville to Chicago, Apr 25-26), Kevin Tran (recReloc00000003, Vectral Systems, Portland to Denver, Apr 19-20), Danielle Osei (recReloc00000004, Vectral Systems, Phoenix to Denver, Apr 26-27), Priya Venkatesh (recReloc00000005, GreenStack Energy, Houston to Seattle, Apr 21-23), Noah Fitzgerald (recReloc00000006, GreenStack Energy, Philadelphia to Seattle, Apr 28-29), Ines Kovac (recReloc00000007, PivotPoint Labs, Brooklyn to Austin, Apr 22-24), Jordan Ekwueme (recReloc00000008, BrightLoop Analytics, San Diego to Boston, Apr 25-26), Andre Washington (recReloc00000009, Mosaic Robotics, Detroit to Atlanta, Apr 26-27), Tom Lindqvist (recReloc00000024, Greenleaf Design Studio, Portland to Denver, Apr 7-9), Daniela Cruz (recReloc00000025, Sunbelt Venture Partners, Chicago to Miami, Apr 14-16), Marco Silva (recReloc00000026, Tideway Hospitality Tech, Tampa to Austin, Apr 7-9), Dana Kowalczyk (recReloc00000027, Tideway Hospitality Tech, Tampa to Seattle, Apr 10-12), Ravi Sundaram (recReloc00000028, Tideway Hospitality Tech, Tampa to Denver, Apr 14-16), and Zara Okoye (recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta, Mosaic Robotics, Savannah to Atlanta, Apr 14-16). Note that multiple records have modified_time set well before their Move End Date: among the first 9, Marchetti, Osei, Fitzgerald, Ekwueme, and Washington were all modified in early April but have move dates in late April. All 6 additional records (Lindqvist, Cruz, Silva, Kowalczyk, Sundaram, Okoye) also have modified_time equal to their created_time, set before their move dates. This premature completion marking is an early signal that the Completed status may not reflect actual move outcomes.
OE 3: Search emails for Lily Marchetti using search_emails (query: "Lily Marchetti" or "Marchetti"). Their Airtable modified_time aligns with their move end dates, and email threads show standard successful completion workflows. For Andre Washington, discover conflicting information: one email thread (email_mosaic_washington_intro, email_mosaic_washington_reply, email_mosaic_washington_details) describes his relocation as Detroit, MI to Atlanta, GA (matching Airtable), but a parallel set of emails (email_relocation_request_001, email_am_ack_001, email_flight_request_001, email_flight_confirm_001, email_client_confirm_001) describes the same person relocating from Columbus, GA to Seattle, WA with flights booked CMH to SEA -- a major geography contradiction. For Noah Fitzgerald, find planning and booking emails (apartment options, flight booking PHL to SEA) but no evidence of actual move completion -- his move dates (Apr 28-29) have not yet occurred as of the current date (April 26, 2026), and his record was marked Completed on April 2. For Jordan Ekwueme, find temp housing coordination and planning emails but note that his move end date is April 26 (today) and the record was marked Completed on April 1, well before the move could have finished.
OE 8: Search emails and Slack for the 6 additional April relocatees using search_emails and conversations_search_messages. For Zara Okoye (Mosaic Robotics, Savannah to Atlanta), discover extensive email threads documenting a major prototype damage incident: Tamara Byrd's formal damage complaint reporting a cracked servo motor housing and misaligned joint calibration on a $90,000 robotic arm prototype (Unit #1) upon delivery on April 15, Emeka Diallo's incident response and full chain-of-custody report to Tamara, and related Slack escalation threads in #operations (C006) including Chloe Vance's chain-of-custody investigation, a delivery receipt signed by an intern (Kyle Britten) instead of Zara, and Marcus Thorne's financial analysis identifying a $40,000 uncovered insurance gap. Although Zara's relocation was physically completed, the move was NOT clean -- the prompt instruction "I don't want to email someone congratulating them if their move is still messy behind the scenes" applies directly to this case. For the remaining 5 additional relocatees -- Tom Lindqvist (Greenleaf Design Studio), Daniela Cruz (Sunbelt Venture Partners), Marco Silva, Dana Kowalczyk, and Ravi Sundaram (all Tideway Hospitality Tech) -- search emails and Slack and find NO correspondence, NO relocation threads, and NO cross-service data to verify their Completed status. Their Airtable records exist but cannot be corroborated through any other service, making cross-checking impossible as required by the prompt.
OE 9: Search Slack for discussions about the remaining 7 relocatees from the first 9 using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Abdi" or "Venkatesh" or "Kovac" or "Fitzgerald" or "Ekwueme" or "Washington" or "Tran"). Discover the Q1/Early Q2 Relocation Volume Update post in #operations (C006) which lists all 9 April relocations with status indicators: Abdi to Chicago (fully booked), Tran to Denver (fully booked), Venkatesh to Seattle (fully booked), Kovac to Austin (fully booked), Marchetti to Chicago (in progress), Osei to Denver (in progress), Fitzgerald to Seattle (in progress), Ekwueme to Boston (in progress), and Washington to Atlanta (in progress). The in-progress markers for Marchetti and Osei provide additional Slack confirmation of their non-completion (corroborating the cancellation evidence from OEs 3-5). The in-progress markers for Fitzgerald, Ekwueme, and Washington directly contradict the Airtable "Completed" status for those three records. Also find the "Two Noahs" confusion thread in #operations where Fatimah asks whether a new intake is "Fitzpatrick or Fitzgerald" -- confirming parallel Seattle moves that could cause mix-ups. For the 4 clean relocatees (Abdi, Tran, Venkatesh, Kovac), find no negative signals in Slack.
OE 10: Update the Lily Marchetti Airtable record to fix the incorrect Completed status using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: recReloc00000002, fields: {Status: "Cancelled"}}]). This corrects the false Completed status based on clear cancellation evidence from emails and the second Airtable record.
OE 11: Update the Danielle Osei Airtable record to fix the incorrect Completed status using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: recReloc00000004, fields: {Status: "Cancelled"}}]). This corrects the false Completed status based on clear cancellation evidence from emails and the second Airtable record.
OE 12: Flag Omar Ibrahim and Julian Brooks about the uncertain records where the evidence is ambiguous, conflicting, or absent rather than conclusive. Use send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com, recipients: ["omar.ibrahim@moveops.com", "julian.brooks@moveops.com"]) or conversations_add_message to a DM. The flag should cover: (a) Andre Washington (recReloc00000009) -- Airtable says Detroit, MI to Atlanta, GA but multiple email threads describe his relocation as Columbus, GA to Seattle, WA with flights booked CMH to SEA; the geography data is contradictory across email and Airtable and the record was marked Completed on April 2 despite a move end date of April 27; (b) Noah Fitzgerald (recReloc00000006) -- move dates are April 28-29 which have not yet occurred, the Slack volume post marks him as in progress, the record was marked Completed on April 2 (26 days before the move starts), and there is a "Two Noahs" confusion risk with a concurrent Axiom/Fitzpatrick Seattle move; (c) Jordan Ekwueme (recReloc00000008) -- the Slack volume post marks him as in progress, the record was marked Completed on April 1 despite a move end date of April 26, and his stipend for a monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) is still Pending Approval with a PDF template fraud flag; (d) Zara Okoye (recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta) -- relocation physically completed but a $90,000 robotic arm prototype was damaged in transit with an ongoing insurance dispute and $40K uncovered gap, so a congratulatory check-in would be inappropriate given the active damage claim; (e) Tom Lindqvist (recReloc00000024), Daniela Cruz (recReloc00000025), Marco Silva (recReloc00000026), Dana Kowalczyk (recReloc00000027), and Ravi Sundaram (recReloc00000028) -- all marked Completed with premature modified_times but no email, Slack, or contact data exists to cross-verify their status, making the prompt's required cross-check impossible. Per the prompt instruction: "If you are not sure, flag me and Julian with what you found instead of editing."
OE 13: Look up contact information for each of the 4 verified clean relocatees using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Yusuf Abdi"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Kevin Tran"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Priya Venkatesh"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Ines Kovac") to retrieve their email addresses before sending check-in emails. Expected addresses: yusuf.abdi@canopyhealth.io, kevin.tran@vectralsystems.com, priya.venkatesh@greenstackenergy.com, ines@pivotpointlabs.com.
OE 14: Send check-in emails from Omar Ibrahim to each of the 4 verified clean relocatees using send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com). Each email should be a short, friendly check-in asking how they are settling in and whether they need anything. Recipients: yusuf.abdi@canopyhealth.io, kevin.tran@vectralsystems.com, priya.venkatesh@greenstackenergy.com, ines@pivotpointlabs.com. Emails should NOT be sent to Lily Marchetti or Danielle Osei (relocations cancelled), should NOT be sent to Noah Fitzgerald, Jordan Ekwueme, or Andre Washington (flagged as uncertain -- awaiting review by Omar and Julian), should NOT be sent to Zara Okoye (active damage incident makes a congratulatory check-in inappropriate), and should NOT be sent to the 5 additional relocatees (Lindqvist, Cruz, Silva, Kowalczyk, Sundaram) whose status cannot be cross-verified.
OE 15: Post a summary message in #customer-support using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C007) listing: (a) who was contacted with check-in emails (Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Kevin Tran, Priya Venkatesh, Ines Kovac -- 4 relocatees whose moves checked out clean across Airtable, email, and Slack); (b) which records were corrected (Lily Marchetti changed from Completed to Cancelled based on cancellation emails and second Airtable record, Danielle Osei changed from Completed to Cancelled based on cancellation emails and second Airtable record); (c) which records were flagged for Omar and Julian's review (Andre Washington -- conflicting geography across Airtable and email, Noah Fitzgerald -- move dates not yet reached and Slack shows in progress, Jordan Ekwueme -- Slack shows in progress with pending stipend fraud flag, Zara Okoye -- move completed but $90K prototype damaged in transit with active insurance dispute, and 5 additional relocatees with no cross-service data available for verification); and (d) the evidence basis for each correction and flag.

All ask about settling in and whether they need anything.

tool selection

✓Run #6The agent used airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables (twice), and airtable_search_records to look up relocation records from the Airtable database, fully satisfying this criterion.
